title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: If x+1x=‚ąí1x + \frac{1}{x} = -1x+x1‚Äč=‚ąí1‚Äč , then compute:x5+1x5+4x3+4x3 x^5 + \frac{1}{x^5} + 4x^3 + \frac{4}{x^3}x5+x51‚Äč+4x3+x34‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äčx+1x=‚ąí1x + \frac{1}{x} = -1x+x1‚Äč=‚ąí1‚Äč‚Äč Concept Used: If x+1x=‚ąí1x + \frac{1}{x} = -1x+x1‚Äč=‚ąí1‚Äč, then x3=1x^3 = 1x3=1‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used: ‚Äčx2+1x2=(x+1x)2‚ąí2x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2} = \left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^2 - 2x2+x21‚Äč=(x+x1‚Äč)2‚ąí2‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Since x3x^3x3‚Äč = 1, then x5=x2x^5 = x^2x5=x2‚Äč and 1x5=1x2\frac{1}{x^5} = \frac{1}{x^2}x51‚Äč=x21‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč x2+1x2x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2}x2+x21‚Äč‚Äč = (‚ąí1)2(-1)^2 (‚ąí1)2‚Äč- 2 = -1 Also, 4x3+4x34x^3 + \frac{4}{x^3}4x3+x34‚Äč‚Äč = 4(1) + 4(1) = 8‚Äč‚Äč Total Value = -1 + 8 = 7 So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A conical vessel is cut at one-fourth its height from the top, parallel to its base. What is the ratio of the volume of the top part to the bottom frustum part?
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äčh=14Hh = \frac{1}{4}Hh=41‚ÄčH‚Äč‚Äč Concept Used: Ratio of volumes of similar cones = (Ratio of heights)3 Formula Used: ‚ÄčV1V2=(h1h2)3\frac{V1}{V2} = \left(\frac{h1}{h2}\right)^3V2V1‚Äč=(h2h1‚Äč)3‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Ratio of small cone to big cone = (14)3\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)^3(41‚Äč)3‚Äč = 164\frac{1}{64}641‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Bottom part (frustum) = 64 - 1 = 63. Ratio = 1 : 63. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A sector of a circle has a central angle of 60¬į and a radius of 12 cm. Another sector of the same circle has a central angle of ŌÄ/2\pi/2 ŌÄ/2 radians. What is the ratio of the area of the first sector to the area of the second sector?
questions.explanation: Given: Central angle of first sector = 60¬į Central angle of second sector = ŌÄ/2 radians Radius = 12 cm Formula used: Area of a sector = (őł / 2) √ó r¬≤ where őł is in radians Solution: Convert 60¬į into radians 60¬į = ŌÄ/3 Now apply formula Area‚āĀ = ( (ŌÄ/3) / 2 ) √ó 12¬≤ Area‚āā = ( (ŌÄ/2) / 2 ) √ó 12¬≤ Taking ratio, common factors 12¬≤ and 1/2 cancel Area‚āĀ : Area‚āā = (ŌÄ/3) : (ŌÄ/2) = 1/3 : 1/2 = 2 : 3 Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Two right circular cylinders have the same diagonal length of 25 cm. The difference between their radii is 2 cm. If the difference between the squares of their heights is 176 cm¬≤, then what is the difference between their Curved Surface Areas (CSA)?
questions.explanation: ‚Äč‚ÄčFormula Used Diagonal of cylinder: D2=(2r)2+h2=4r2+h2D^2 = (2r)^2 + h^2 = 4r^2 + h^2D2=(2r)2+h2=4r2+h2‚Äč‚Äč CSA = 2ŌÄrh2 \pi r h2ŌÄrh‚Äč‚Äč Solution 1. From diagonal formula: ‚Äč4r12+h12=252=6254r22+h22=6254r_1^2 + h_1^2 = 25^2 = 625 \\4r_2^2 + h_2^2 = 6254r12‚Äč+h12‚Äč=252=6254r22‚Äč+h22‚Äč=625‚Äč‚Äč 2. Subtract the two equations: ‚Äč(4r22+h22)‚ąí(4r12+h12)=04(r22‚ąír12)=h12‚ąíh224(r22‚ąír12)=176r22‚ąír12=44(r2‚ąír1)(r2+r1)=44(4r_2^2 + h_2^2) - (4r_1^2 + h_1^2) = 0 \\ 4(r_2^2 - r_1^2) = h_1^2 - h_2^2\\ 4(r_2^2 - r_1^2) = 176\\ r_2^2 - r_1^2 = 44\\ (r_2 - r_1)(r_2 + r_1) = 44\\ (4r22‚Äč+h22‚Äč)‚ąí(4r12‚Äč+h12‚Äč)=04(r22‚Äč‚ąír12‚Äč)=h12‚Äč‚ąíh22‚Äč4(r22‚Äč‚ąír12‚Äč)=176r22‚Äč‚ąír12‚Äč=44(r2‚Äč‚ąír1‚Äč)(r2‚Äč+r1‚Äč)=44‚Äč‚Äč Since r2‚ąír1=2,r_2 - r_1 = 2,r2‚Äč‚ąír1‚Äč=2,‚Äč then: ‚Äč2(r2+r1)=44=&gtr2+r1=222(r_2 + r_1) = 44 \Rightarrow r_2 + r_1 = 222(r2‚Äč+r1‚Äč)=44=&gtr2‚Äč+r1‚Äč=22‚Äč‚Äč Find radii: ‚Äčr2+r1=22r2‚ąír1=2r_2 + r_1 = 22 \\r_2 - r_1 = 2r2‚Äč+r1‚Äč=22r2‚Äč‚ąír1‚Äč=2‚Äč‚Äč Adding: 2r2=24=&gtr2=12 2r_2 = 24 \Rightarrow r_2 = 122r2‚Äč=24=&gtr2‚Äč=12‚Äč‚Äč Subtracting: 2r1=20=&gtr1=102r_1 = 20 \Rightarrow r_1 = 102r1‚Äč=20=&gtr1‚Äč=10‚Äč‚Äč 4. Find heights: ‚Äčh12=625‚ąí4(10)2=625‚ąí400=225=&gth1=15h22=625‚ąí4(12)2=625‚ąí576=49=&gth2=7h_1^2 = 625 - 4(10)^2 = 625 - 400 = 225 \Rightarrow h_1 = 15 \\h_2^2 = 625 - 4(12)^2 = 625 - 576 = 49 \Rightarrow h_2 = 7h12‚Äč=625‚ąí4(10)2=625‚ąí400=225=&gth1‚Äč=15h22‚Äč=625‚ąí4(12)2=625‚ąí576=49=&gth2‚Äč=7‚Äč‚Äč 5. Diff in CSA: ‚ÄčCSA1=2ŌÄ(10)(15)=300ŌÄCSA2=2ŌÄ(12)(7)=168ŌÄCSA_1 = 2\pi(10)(15) = 300\pi \\CSA_2 = 2\pi(12)(7) = 168\piCSA1‚Äč=2ŌÄ(10)(15)=300ŌÄCSA2‚Äč=2ŌÄ(12)(7)=168ŌÄ‚Äč‚Äč Difference = 300ŌÄ‚ąí168ŌÄ=132ŌÄ300\pi - 168\pi = 132\pi300ŌÄ‚ąí168ŌÄ=132ŌÄ‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: In a circular race of 1000 m, Ravi and Sumit start from the same point and at the same time, running at speeds of 9 km/hr and 27 km/hr, respectively. When will they meet again for the first time on the track if they are running in the same direction?
questions.explanation: Given Distance = 1000 m = 1 km Speeds: 9 km/hr and 27 km/hr (Same direction) Formula Used Time = DistanceRelative Speed \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Relative Speed}}Relative SpeedDistance‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Relative Speed (Same Direction) = S1 - S2 Solution Relative Speed = 27 - 9 = 18 km/hr Convert to m/s: 18√ó518=5 m/s18 \times \frac{5}{18} = 5 \text{ m/s}18√ó185‚Äč=5 m/s‚Äč‚Äč Time = 10005=200 seconds \frac{1000}{5} = 200 \text{ seconds}51000‚Äč=200 seconds‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A company's stock price increases by x% in the first quarter and decreases by the same x% in the second quarter. If the final stock price is 14% lower than the initial price, what is the value of x? (Round off the answer to two decimal places.)
questions.explanation: Given First change = +x% Second change = -x% Net result = -14% (decrease) Formula Used Net percentage change = A + B + A√óB100 \frac{A \times B}{100}100A√óB‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution Here A = x and B = -x. Net Change = x - x - x2100\frac{x^2}{100}100x2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč‚ąí14=‚ąíx2100 14=x2100 x2=1400 x=1400 x=1014 Using14‚Čą3.7416: x‚Čą10√ó3.7416=37.416 -14 = -\frac{x^2}{100} \\\ \\14 = \frac{x^2}{100} \\\ \\x^2 = 1400 \\\ \\x = \sqrt{1400} \\\ \\x = 10\sqrt{14} \\\ \\Using \sqrt{14} \approx 3.7416: \\\ \\x \approx 10 \times 3.7416 = 37.416 \\\ \\‚ąí14=‚ąí100x2‚Äč 14=100x2‚Äč x2=1400 x=1400‚Äč x=1014‚Äč Using14‚Äč‚Čą3.7416: x‚Čą10√ó3.7416=37.416 ‚Äč‚Äč Rounding to two decimal places: 37.42 Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: The capacity of a cylindrical vessel is 2,94,375 cm¬≥. If its base diameter is 50 cm, then what is the height (in cm) of the cylindrical vessel? (Use ŌÄ = 3.14.)
questions.explanation: Given Volume of vessel (V) = 2,94,375 cm¬≥ Diameter = 50 cm Radius (r) = 25 cm ŌÄ = 3.14 Formula Used Volume of cylinder = ŌÄr2h \pi r^2 hŌÄr2h‚Äč‚Äč Solution Substituting the values in the formula: ‚Äč294375=3.14√ó(25)2√óh 294375=3.14√ó625√óh 294375=1962.5√óh h=2943751962.5 h=150 cm 294375 = 3.14 \times (25)^2 \times h \\\ \\294375 = 3.14 \times 625 \times h \\\ \\294375 = 1962.5 \times h \\\ \\h = \frac{294375}{1962.5} \\\ \\h = 150 \text{ cm} \\\ \\294375=3.14√ó(25)2√óh 294375=3.14√ó625√óh 294375=1962.5√óh h=1962.5294375‚Äč h=150 cm ‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A pipe can fill a tank in 8 hours. Another pipe can empty the filled tank in 40 hours. If both the pipes are opened simultaneously, then the time (in hours) in which the tank will be half filled, is:
questions.explanation: Given Pipe A (Fill) = 8 hours Pipe B (Empty) = 40 hours Formula Used Total Efficiency = 1A‚ąí1B\frac{1}{A} - \frac{1}{B}A1‚Äč‚ąíB1‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution Efficiency of Pipe A = 18 \frac{1}{8}81‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Efficiency of Pipe B = 140\frac{1}{40}401‚Äč‚Äč Combined Efficiency = 18‚ąí140=5‚ąí140=440=110 \frac{1}{8} - \frac{1}{40} = \frac{5 - 1}{40} = \frac{4}{40} = \frac{1}{10}81‚Äč‚ąí401‚Äč=405‚ąí1‚Äč=404‚Äč=101‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Time to fill the full tank = 10 hours. Time to fill half the tank = 102=5 hours.\frac{10}{2} = 5 \text{ hours}.210‚Äč=5 hours.‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Two workers, A and B, working together can complete a task in 14 days. A alone can complete the same task in 24 days. They start the work together, but after 5 days, A falls sick and B completes the remaining work alone. In how many total days was the work completed?
questions.explanation: Given Time (A + B) = 14 days Time (A) = 24 days Solution Let Total Work = LCM(14, 24) = 168 units. Efficiency (A + B) = 168 / 14 = 12 units/day Efficiency (A) = 168 / 24 = 7 units/day Efficiency (B) = 12 - 7 = 5 units/day Work done in first 5 days (A + B) = 12 √ó5 \times 5√ó5‚Äč = 60 units. Remaining work = 168 - 60 = 108 units. Time for B to finish remaining work = 108 / 5 = 21.6 days. Total Time = 5 + 21.6 = 26.6 days. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: The cost price of an article is ‚āĻ420, and its marked price is ‚āĻ1,120. If the seller gives a discount of 55% on the marked price, what will be his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Given CP = 420 MP = 1120 Discount = 55% Formula Used SP = MP√ó(1‚ąíD100)MP \times (1 - \frac{D}{100})MP√ó(1‚ąí100D‚Äč)‚Äč‚Äč Profit %=SP‚ąíCPCP√ó100\% = \frac{SP - CP}{CP} \times 100%=CPSP‚ąíCP‚Äč√ó100‚Äč‚Äč Solution SP = 1120√ó(1‚ąí0.55)=1120√ó0.451120 \times (1 - 0.55) = 1120 \times 0.451120√ó(1‚ąí0.55)=1120√ó0.45‚Äč‚Äč SP = 1120√ó920=56√ó9=5041120 \times \frac{9}{20} = 56 \times 9 = 5041120√ó209‚Äč=56√ó9=504‚Äč‚Äč Profit = 504 - 420 = 84 ‚ÄčProfit % =84420√ó100=20% \text{Profit \% }= \frac{84}{420} \times 100 = 20\% \\\ \\Profit % =42084‚Äč√ó100=20% ‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: If (a + b):(a - b) = 7:3 and (x + y):(x - y) = 8:5, find the ratio of a/x : b/y.
questions.explanation: Given ‚Äča+ba‚ąíb=73 x+yx‚ąíy=85\frac{a+b}{a-b} = \frac{7}{3} \\\ \\\frac{x+y}{x-y} = \frac{8}{5}a‚ąíba+b‚Äč=37‚Äč x‚ąíyx+y‚Äč=58‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used Componendo and Dividendo Rule: ‚ÄčIf AB=CD,thenA+BA‚ąíB=C+DC‚ąíD\text{If} \, \frac{A}{B} = \frac{C}{D}, \text {then} \frac{A+B}{A-B} = \frac{C+D}{C-D}IfBA‚Äč=DC‚Äč,thenA‚ąíBA+B‚Äč=C‚ąíDC+D‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution ‚Äč(a+b)+(a‚ąíb)(a+b)‚ąí(a‚ąíb)=7+37‚ąí3 2a2b=104 ab=52 (x+y)+(x‚ąíy)(x+y)‚ąí(x‚ąíy)=8+58‚ąí5 2x2y=133 xy=133 ‚üĻ yx=313 a/xb/y=ax√óyb=(ab)√ó(yx) =52√ó313=1526 \frac{(a+b)+(a-b)}{(a+b)-(a-b)} = \frac{7+3}{7-3} \\\ \\\frac{2a}{2b} = \frac{10}{4}\\\ \\\frac{a}{b} = \frac{5}{2}\\\ \\\frac{(x+y)+(x-y)}{(x+y)-(x-y)} = \frac{8+5}{8-5}\\\ \\\frac{2x}{2y} = \frac{13}{3}\\\ \\\frac{x}{y} = \frac{13}{3} \implies \frac{y}{x} = \frac{3}{13}\\\ \\\frac{a/x}{b/y} = \frac{a}{x} \times \frac{y}{b} = (\frac{a}{b}) \times (\frac{y}{x})\\\ \\= \frac{5}{2} \times \frac{3}{13} = \frac{15}{26}\\\ \\(a+b)‚ąí(a‚ąíb)(a+b)+(a‚ąíb)‚Äč=7‚ąí37+3‚Äč 2b2a‚Äč=410‚Äč ba‚Äč=25‚Äč (x+y)‚ąí(x‚ąíy)(x+y)+(x‚ąíy)‚Äč=8‚ąí58+5‚Äč 2y2x‚Äč=313‚Äč yx‚Äč=313‚Äč‚üĻxy‚Äč=133‚Äč b/ya/x‚Äč=xa‚Äč√óby‚Äč=(ba‚Äč)√ó(xy‚Äč) =25‚Äč√ó133‚Äč=2615‚Äč ‚Äč‚Äč Ratio is 15 : 26 Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Praveen and Hemant start a business together. Praveen invests ‚āĻ83,000 more than Hemant for 7 months, while Hemant invests for 3 months. Out of a total profit of ‚āĻ5,342, Praveen‚Äôs share exceeds Hemant‚Äôs by ‚āĻ2,671. Find the amount invested by Praveen.
questions.explanation: Given Total Profit = 5342 Praveen's Share - Hemant's Share = 2671 Time: Praveen = 7 months, Hemant = 3 months Praveen's Investment = Hemant's Investment + 83000 Formula Used Ratio of Profit = Ratio of (Investment √ó \times √ó‚ÄčTime) Solution Step 1: Find Profit Shares P + H = 5342 P - H = 2671 Adding equations: 2P = 8013 ‚üĻ \implies‚üĻ‚Äč P = 4006.5 Subtracting: 2H = 2671 ‚üĻ \implies‚üĻ‚Äč H = 1335.5 Ratio P : H = 4006.51335.5=31 \frac{4006.5}{1335.5} = \frac{3}{1}1335.54006.5‚Äč=13‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Let Hemant's Inv = I, Praveen's Inv = I + 83000 ‚Äč(I+83000)√ó7I√ó3=31 \frac{(I + 83000) \times 7}{I \times 3} = \frac{3}{1} \\\ \\I√ó3(I+83000)√ó7‚Äč=13‚Äč 7(I + 83000) = 9I 7I + 581000 = 9I 2I = 581000 I = 290500 Praveen's Investment = 290500 + 83000 = 373500 Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: A man invests ‚āĻ20,000 in three schemes in the ratio 2:3:5. The schemes offer returns of 5%, 10%, and 15% respectively. What is the total income generated?
questions.explanation: Given: Total Investment = ‚āĻ20,000. Ratio = 2:3:5. Sum of ratios = 10. Solution: Investment in Scheme 1 =210√ó20000 \frac{2}{10} \times 20000102‚Äč√ó20000‚Äč = 4000. Investment in Scheme 2 =310√ó20000 \frac{3}{10} \times 20000103‚Äč√ó20000‚Äč = 6000. Investment in Scheme 3 = 510√ó20000\frac{5}{10} \times 20000105‚Äč√ó20000‚Äč = 10000. Income: From 1: 5% of 4000 = 200. From 2: 10% of 6000 = 600. From 3: 15% of 10000 = 1500. Total Income = 200 + 600 + 1500 = ‚āĻ2300. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: A shopkeeper marks his goods to gain 60% on the cost price but allows a discount of 15% on the marked price. What is his actual profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Given: Markup % = 60%. Discount % = 15%. Formula Used: Successive percentage change: A + B + AB100\frac{AB}{100}100AB‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Here A = +60, B = -15. Solution: Profit % = 60 - 15 - 60√ó15100\frac{60 \times 15}{100}10060√ó15‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 45 - 900100\frac{900}{100}100900‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 45 - 9 = 36%. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: In what ratio must water be mixed with milk costing ‚āĻ60 per litre so as to gain 20% by selling the mixture at ‚āĻ54 per litre?
questions.explanation: Given: Cost of Milk = ‚āĻ60/L. Cost of Water = ‚āĻ0/L. Selling Price (SP) of Mixture = ‚āĻ54/L. Gain = 20%. Formula Used: Cost Price (CP) = SP√ó100100+Gain%\frac{SP \times 100}{100 + Gain\%}100+Gain%SP√ó100‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Rule of Alligation. Solution: Find CP of Mixture: CP = 54√ó100120=54012\frac{54 \times 100}{120} = \frac{540}{12}12054√ó100‚Äč=12540‚Äč‚Äč = ‚āĻ45. Apply Alligation: Water (0) : Milk (60) Mean Price (45) Difference for Water: 60 - 45 = 15. Difference for Milk: 45 - 0 = 45. Ratio = 15 : 45 = 1 : 3. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: A person invested ‚āĻ15,000 in two different schemes. He invested one part for 5 years at 4% simple interest and the other part for the same duration at 6% simple interest. If the interest earned from the second part was ‚āĻ1,500 more than the interest earned from the first part, how much money was invested in the second scheme?
questions.explanation: Given: Total investment = ‚āĻ15,000 Scheme 1: Time = 5 years, Rate = 4% Scheme 2: Time = 5 years, Rate = 6% Difference in interest = ‚āĻ1,500 Formula Used: ‚ÄčSI=P√óR√óT100 SI = \frac{P \times R \times T}{100}SI=100P√óR√óT‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let the amount invested in the second scheme be x. Then, the amount invested in the first scheme is 15000 - x. Interest from second scheme: SI2=x√ó6√ó5100=30x100SI_2 = \frac{x \times 6 \times 5}{100} = \frac{30x}{100}SI2‚Äč=100x√ó6√ó5‚Äč=10030x‚Äč ‚Äč‚ÄčInterest from first scheme: SI1=(15000‚ąíx)√ó4√ó5100=20(15000‚ąíx)100 SI_1 = \frac{(15000 - x) \times 4 \times 5}{100} = \frac{20(15000 - x)}{100}SI1‚Äč=100(15000‚ąíx)√ó4√ó5‚Äč=10020(15000‚ąíx)‚Äč ‚ÄčAccording to the problem: SI2‚ąíSI1SI_2 - SI_1SI2‚Äč‚ąíSI1‚Äč‚Äč = 1500 ‚Äč30x100‚ąí20(15000‚ąíx)100=1500 \frac{30x}{100} - \frac{20(15000 - x)}{100} = 150010030x‚Äč‚ąí10020(15000‚ąíx)‚Äč=1500‚Äč‚Äč 30x - 300000 + 20x = 150000 50x = 450000 x = 9000 The amount invested in the second scheme is ‚āĻ9,000. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: ‚Ė≥PQR has sides PQ = 8 cm, QR = 10 cm, PR = 12 cm. ‚Ė≥LMN is congruent to ‚Ė≥PQR. What is the perimeter of ‚Ė≥LMN?
questions.explanation: Given Sides of‚Ė≥ \triangle ‚Ė≥‚ÄčPQR: PQ = 8 cm, QR = 10 cm, PR = 12 cm ‚Äč‚Ė≥LMN‚ČÖ‚Ė≥PQR\triangle LMN \cong \triangle PQR‚Ė≥LMN‚ČÖ‚Ė≥PQR‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used 1. Perimeter = Sum of all sides 2. Congruent triangles have equal corresponding sides and perimeter. Solution Perimeter of ‚Ė≥\triangle‚Ė≥‚Äč PQR = 8 + 10 + 12 = 30 cm Since ‚Ė≥\triangle‚Ė≥‚Äč LMN is congruent to ‚Ė≥\triangle‚Ė≥‚Äč PQR, their perimeters must be identical. Perimeter of ‚Ė≥ \triangle‚Ė≥‚Äč LMN = 30 cm Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: If cos‚Ā°A = 4/5 and sin‚Ā°B = 5/13 , A and B‚ąą(0,ŌÄ2) , find cos(A - B).
questions.explanation: Given: cosA = 4/5 sinB = 5/13 A, B ‚ąą (0, ŌÄ/2) Find cos(A ‚ąí B) Concept Used: Trigonometric identity of cosine difference and Pythagoras identity. Formula Used: cos(A ‚ąí B) = cosA cosB + sinA sinB sin¬≤őł + cos¬≤őł = 1 Solution: ‚Äčcos‚Ā°A=45 sin‚Ā°A=1‚ąícos‚Ā°2A =1‚ąí1625 =925 =35 sin‚Ā°B=513 cos‚Ā°B=1‚ąísin‚Ā°2B =1‚ąí25169 =144169 =1213 cos‚Ā°(A‚ąíB)=cos‚Ā°Acos‚Ā°B+sin‚Ā°Asin‚Ā°B =(45√ó1213)+(35√ó513) =4865+1565=6365\cos A = \frac{4}{5}\\ \ \\\sin A = \sqrt{1 - \cos^2 A}\\ \ \\= \sqrt{1 - \frac{16}{25}}\\ \ \\ = \sqrt{\frac{9}{25}}\\ \ \\= \frac{3}{5}\\ \ \\\sin B = \frac{5}{13}\\ \ \\\cos B = \sqrt{1 - \sin^2 B}\\ \ \\= \sqrt{1 - \frac{25}{169}}\\ \ \\= \sqrt{\frac{144}{169}}\\ \ \\= \frac{12}{13}\\ \ \\\cos(A - B) = \cos A \cos B + \sin A \sin B\\ \ \\= \left(\frac{4}{5} \times \frac{12}{13}\right) + \left(\frac{3}{5} \times \frac{5}{13}\right)\\ \ \\= \frac{48}{65} + \frac{15}{65}= \frac{63}{65}cosA=54‚Äč sinA=1‚ąícos2A‚Äč =1‚ąí2516‚Äč‚Äč =259‚Äč‚Äč =53‚Äč sinB=135‚Äč cosB=1‚ąísin2B‚Äč =1‚ąí16925‚Äč‚Äč =169144‚Äč‚Äč =1312‚Äč cos(A‚ąíB)=cosAcosB+sinAsinB =(54‚Äč√ó1312‚Äč)+(53‚Äč√ó135‚Äč) =6548‚Äč+6515‚Äč=6563‚Äč ‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: If a = 5\sqrt{5}5‚Äč‚Äč and b = 5 -5 \sqrt{5}5‚Äč‚Äč, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: Given: a = 5 \sqrt{5}5‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč b = 5 - 5\sqrt{5}5‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Calculate a + b: a + b =5+(5‚ąí5) \sqrt{5} + (5 - \sqrt{5})5‚Äč+(5‚ąí5‚Äč)‚Äč = 5. Since 5 is a rational number, a + b is rational. Check other options: a - b =5‚ąí(5‚ąí5)=25‚ąí5 \sqrt{5} - (5 - \sqrt{5}) = 2\sqrt{5} - 55‚Äč‚ąí(5‚ąí5‚Äč)=25‚Äč‚ąí5‚Äč (Irrational). ab =5(5‚ąí5)=55‚ąí5 \sqrt{5}(5 - \sqrt{5}) = 5\sqrt{5} - 55‚Äč(5‚ąí5‚Äč)=55‚Äč‚ąí5‚Äč (Irrational). Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: A man invests ‚āĻ10,000 at 10% compound interest per annum. After 1 year, he adds ‚āĻ5,000 more. What will be the total amount at the end of 2 years from the start?
questions.explanation: Given: Initial P = 10,000. Rate = 10%. After 1 year, adds 5,000. Solution: Amount after 1 year = 10,000 √ó 1.10 = 11,000. New Principal for 2nd year = 11,000 + 5,000 = 16,000. Interest for 2nd year = 16,000 √ó 10% = 1,600. Total Amount at end of year 2 = 16,000 + 1,600 = 17,600. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Working 6 hours a day, Raman can complete a piece of work in 15 days and working 8 hours a day Mohan can complete the same work in 10 days. In how many days would they complete the work, if working together 10 hours in a day?
questions.explanation: Given Raman's total hours = 15√ó6=90 15 \times 6 = 9015√ó6=90‚Äč hours Mohan's total hours = 10√ó8=8010 \times 8 = 8010√ó8=80‚Äč hours Combined working hours per day = 10 hours Formula Used Time taken together = A√óBA+B \frac{A \times B}{A + B}A+BA√óB‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution Total hours needed together =90√ó8090+80=7200170=72017 \frac{90 \times 80}{90 + 80} = \frac{7200}{170} = \frac{720}{17} 90+8090√ó80‚Äč=1707200‚Äč=17720‚Äč‚Äčhours Working 10 hours a day, number of days: Days = 720/1710=7217\frac{720/17}{10} = \frac{72}{17}10720/17‚Äč=1772‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Days =4417 4 \frac{4}{17}4174‚Äč‚Äčdays Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Rohan in his football career, scored 8 goals on an average in 10 matches. If he scored 12 goals on an average in the first 5 matches and 6 goals on an average in the last 3 matches, then find the average of the goals scored by him per match in the remaining 2 matches.
questions.explanation: Given Average goals in 10 matches = 8 Average goals in first 5 matches = 12 Average goals in last 3 matches = 6 Formula Used Total = Average √ó Number of items Remaining Average = Remaining TotalRemaining Matches\frac{\text{Remaining Total}}{\text{Remaining Matches}}Remaining MatchesRemaining Total‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution Total goals in 10 matches = 10√ó8=8010 \times 8 = 8010√ó8=80‚Äč‚Äč Total goals in first 5 matches = 5√ó12=605 \times 12 = 605√ó12=60‚Äč Total goals in last 3 matches = 3√ó6=183 \times 6 = 183√ó6=18‚Äč‚Äč Total goals in 8 matches = 60 + 18 = 78 Goals in remaining 2 matches = 80 - 78 = 2 Average of remaining 2 matches = 22\frac{2}{2}22‚Äč‚Äč = 1goal Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Assertion (A): Two circles touching each other externally have exactly three common tangents. Reason (R): One direct common tangent and two transverse common tangents can be drawn.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
questions.explanation: Given: Assertion (A): Two circles touching externally have 3 common tangents. Reason (R): One direct and two transverse tangents. Solution: When two circles touch each other externally: 1. They have two direct common tangents. 2. They have one transverse common tangent passing through the point of contact. Total common tangents = 3. So, Assertion (A) is True. Reason (R) states "One direct and two transverse", which is incorrect. It should be "Two direct and one transverse". So, Reason (R) is False. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Given, x+1x=3 x + \frac{1}{x} = 3x+x1‚Äč=3‚Äč , Find the value of: (x6+1x6)‚ąí2(x4+1x4)+3(x2+1x2)(x^6 + \frac{1}{x^6}) - 2(x^4 + \frac{1}{x^4}) + 3(x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2})(x6+x61‚Äč)‚ąí2(x4+x41‚Äč)+3(x2+x21‚Äč)‚Äč‚Äč

The bar graph shows the sales of cell phones (in thousands) from four branches of a company during two consecutive years 2018 and 2019. What is the average sales of all the branches (in thousands) for the year 2018?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄčGiven: The sales data for 4 branches in year 2018 from the graph. Solution: Sum of sales for all 4 branches in 2018=60 (in thousands). Average Sales=Sum of SalesNumber of Branches Average Sales=604=15. Final Answer 15\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{The sales data for 4 branches in year 2018 from the graph.} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Sum of sales for all 4 branches in 2018} = 60 \text{ (in thousands)}. \\ \space \, \\ \text{Average Sales} = \frac{\text{Sum of Sales}}{\text{Number of Branches}} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Average Sales} = \frac{60}{4} = 15. \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ 15Given: The sales data for 4 branches in year 2018 from the graph. Solution: Sum of sales for all 4 branches in 2018=60 (in thousands). Average Sales=Number of BranchesSum of Sales‚Äč Average Sales=460‚Äč=15. Final Answer 15‚Äč‚Äč
